title: Hello World! 🌍
description: Write and run your very first C program.

slides.title: Your First Program
slides.content:
In C, every program must have a **`main` function**. This is where the program starts executing.

Let's write the famous `Hello World!` program.

slides.title: Hello World in C
slides.content: Take a look at this code. Don't worry if it looks like alien writing; we will break it down line by line next!

slides.title: Breaking it Down: The Library 📚
slides.content:
## #include <stdio.h>

* **`#include`** tells C to add an external file to our program.
* **`stdio.h`** stands for **St**andar**d** **I**nput **O**utput. It's a library that contains functions for printing text and reading input.

Without this line, C wouldn't know how to print "Hello, World!" to the screen.

quizData.question: What is the purpose of `#include <stdio.h>`?

options.text: To tell the computer to start the main function.
To hide errors from the screen.
To import basic input/output functions like printf.

quizData.explanation: If we don't include <stdio.h>, the compiler won't know what `printf` means.

slides.title: Breaking it Down: The Heart ❤️
slides.content:
## int main() { ... }

* **`int`**: Stands for `integer`. It tells the computer that this function will return a whole number back to the system when it finishes.
* **`main()`**: This is the heart of every C program. Execution *always* begins inside the `main` block.
* **`{` and `}`**: These curly braces enclose the code that belongs to `main`.

slides.title: Breaking it Down: The Action 💥
slides.content:
## printf("Hello, World!");

* **`printf()`**: This is a function that *prints formatted text* to the screen.
* Notice the double quotes around the text.
* **Important**: In C, almost every statement must end with a **semicolon (`;`)**. It's like the period at the end of a sentence.

quizData.question: What symbol MUST appear at the end of most statements in C, like `printf`?
quizData.explanation: The semicolon tells the C compiler that the instruction is finished.

slides.title: Breaking it Down: The Exit 🚪
Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content:
## Missing Semicolons

The most common error beginners make in C is forgetting the semicolon! If your program refuses to run and yells complicated errors at you, check the end of your lines first.
